title: Static accessible dashboard over the open dataset (provenance + uncertainty shown)
context: A static, accessible dashboard over the open dataset makes the screening-coverage trends visually accessible to public-health staff, journalists, and data reusers without requiring them to process raw files. Because the dashboard is built on the open dataset and shows population-level aggregate statistics, it is medium risk rather than high — it does not give medical advice and is not patient-facing in the same way as the written explainers. However, every chart must be provenance-linked, must show uncertainty, must not imply causation, and must meet WCAG 2.2 AA. Depends on M2 indicators (011) and M3 explainers (015) for chart context.
objective: Build a static, accessible dashboard over the open cancer-screening-coverage trend dataset: provenance-linked charts with uncertainty shown, no causal/advice implications, WCAG 2.2 AA, works without a backend. Charts display the population-level aggregate trend data and link to the explainers for interpretation context.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard is a static site (no runtime backend, no server-side state, no database of people, no PII) built only on the committed open dataset
Every chart is provenance-linked: each visualization includes a visible source citation linking to the specific dataset series and its provenance record
Uncertainty shown on every trend chart: confidence/credible intervals displayed; suppressed cells shown as suppressed (not as zero or as an estimated value)

No chart implies causation: chart titles and labels use descriptive language ('Breast screening coverage trend, US 2010-2023') not causal language ('Why coverage fell'); no causal narrative in axis labels or chart annotations
No chart or label gives medical advice, personalized screening recommendations, or eligibility guidance; population-level framing throughout
WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility requirements met: color contrast, keyboard navigation, screen-reader compatibility, alternative text for all charts
Dashboard works without a backend and can be deployed as a static site (e.g., GitHub Pages); no credentials or API keys required at runtime
Statistical and accessibility review passed; sign-off recorded before merge
output: A merged PR adding a static accessible dashboard over the open cancer-screening-coverage trend dataset: all charts provenance-linked, uncertainty displayed, no causal/advice implications, WCAG 2.2 AA met, no backend required; statistical and accessibility review sign-offs recorded.
